q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: urllib.request.urlopen(url) on line 15 is network I/O and an SSRF sink that contacts a user-controlled URL.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Not evaluated; decision stopped at Q1.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated; decision stopped at Q1.

mock_guidance.target: Mock or patch urllib.request.urlopen to intercept the outbound HTTP request triggered by the user-supplied URL argument.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the full url string passed to urlopen to verify it matches the malicious payload that bypasses the startswith('https://insecurewebapp.com') check (e.g., contains redirect or subdomain tricks).
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a mock HTTP response object with a .read() method returning a fixed byte string and a .status attribute (e.g., 200) so that the calling code can proceed to the html variable assignment without error.
notes: The vulnerability is in the prefix-only sanitization on line 14; the mock must allow execution to reach urlopen but prevent actual network activity.
Use unittest.mock.patch on urllib.request.urlopen or patch the function's module-level reference.
